drop_log.reason: Confirmed false positive: UnsafeClaimsWithoutVerification is inside Test() probe function (routing classifier, ext_jwt.go:346), not an authentication decision point. Authentication uses verified claims in authenticateAsService() and authenticateAsUserViaOBO(). SINK-011 annotation corroborates. Not a security boundary crossing.

Generic ORM internal sink (session_raw.go:174). No specific attacker-controlled source-to-sink path demonstrated in taint slice — the 8 raw CodeQL findings flag ORM internals generically. xorm uses parameterized args for user data in normal callers. Requires per-call-site validation not achievable without additional probe work. Downgraded to correctness/robustness audit item.

Path injection at static.go:143 uses http.FileSystem.Open() which is a virtual filesystem abstraction, not OS filesystem directly. Path traversal to OS level is blocked at the FileSystem layer. Companion open redirect (SAST-020) is also mitigated: lines 164-170 apply regexp rePrefix to strip leading // and /\ sequences before http.Redirect. Both halves of the CodeQL finding have confirmed mitigations.

Reflected XSS at response_writer.go:100 is a raw middleware write wrapper. No source-to-sink slice provided — all actual XSS depends on individual endpoint Content-Type handling. Generic sink without a concrete attack path. Effect is same-user and not demonstrably cross-boundary without per-endpoint analysis.

SHA256 for session token lookup is standard practice and not password hashing. hashToken(secretKey, token) adds HMAC-like binding to the secret key. Token source is crypto/rand. Low severity equivalent — dropped per policy.

Go 1.22+ defaults to TLS 1.2 minimum making the missing MinVersion a defense-in-depth gap only. Active only when RendererCACert is configured. Requires network-level attacker. Low severity equivalent — dropped per policy.

False positive: os.Create filePath at http_mode.go:191 originates from getNewFilePath() (confirmed at line 95 via call trace), which generates a server-side random 20-character temp path. The path is not derived from renderer response body or headers. nolint:gosec suppression is correct here.

renderMarkdown(content) at GenAISuggestionsDrawer.tsx:114 calls sanitizeTextPanelContent() (xss library) by default. The noSanitize option is not passed at this call site. The theoretical prompt-injection to XSS chain requires noSanitize=true which is absent. Low-medium severity equivalent — dropped.

Open redirect at static.go:164 is mitigated: after fmt.Sprintf appends a slash, the else branch at line 168-170 applies regexp rePrefix (^(?:/\\|/+)) to collapse leading // and /\ before redirect. Protocol-relative URL bypass via leading // is specifically handled.
LOW severity — dropped per policy. SHA256 for external session token lookup; same analysis as SAST-014.
LOW severity — dropped per policy. OAuth token SHA256; informational without context to distinguish token-lookup vs password-hashing.
LOW severity — dropped per policy. Reflected XSS in custom_route_metrics.go likely false positive; metrics handlers return non-HTML content types.

Duplicate coverage: SQL Expression INTO OUTFILE IsReadOnly bypass is the same root finding as SAST-001/SAST-002 (CVE-2024-9264). Retained as EF-015 (spec-level evidence) consolidated with EF-001 rather than as a separate finding to avoid double-counting in Phase 8 chambers.

Defense-in-depth gap only. CSP is disabled by default (content_security_policy = false in defaults.ini). Does not independently enable new attacks — requires a pre-existing XSS injection point AND operator explicitly enabling CSP believing it provides protection. Dropped as defense-in-depth-only per policy.

findings.title: SQL Expression Engine: SELECT INTO OUTFILE Arbitrary File Write (CVE-2024-9264)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 03 — CRITICAL (4-control failure: INTO allowlisted + IsReadOnly bypassed + DisableFileWrites absent + secure_file_priv empty)
findings.attacker_control: Authenticated user (Editor+) controls the SQL expression string in POST /api/ds/query body when sqlExpressions feature flag is enabled by admin
findings.runtime: Go server process — go-mysql-server runs in-process sharing the Grafana OS process context and filesystem permissions
findings.trust_boundary: TB-10 (SQL Expression Engine in-process). User SQL expression crosses from query execution context into OS filesystem write capability. Feature flag gates entry but does not constrain the write path once inside.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege: Editor-role user achieves OS-level file write as the Grafana process user. Enables: cron directory backdoor (RCE), grafana.ini overwrite (auth disable), plugin directory injection, datasource data exfiltration to filesystem.
preconditions: sqlExpressions feature flag enabled by admin (non-default)
Authenticated user with Editor or higher role
findings.phase8_priority: CRITICAL — 4 independent control failures; spec gap confirms at library semantics level
findings.remediation: 1) Remove *sqlparser.Into from allowedNode() in parser_allow.go. 2) Add mysql.WithDisableFileWrites(true) to mysql.NewContext() at db.go:71. 3) Set secure_file_priv system variable to a restricted path (currently commented out at db.go:76-77).

findings.title: Avatar Endpoint Anonymous Auth Bypass Enables Unauthenticated Outbound SSRF and DoS (CVE-2026-21720)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 04 — HIGH confirmed (Avatar reqSignedIn anonymous bypass)
findings.attacker_control: Unauthenticated attacker (when [auth.anonymous] enabled=true) controls arbitrary :hash value in GET /avatar/:hash at api.go:605
findings.runtime: Go server process — outbound HTTP to secure.gravatar.com per unique hash
findings.trust_boundary: TB-1/TB-2: External unauthenticated attacker bypasses authentication gate. reqSignedIn sets requireLogin = !AllowAnonymous || forceLogin || ReqNoAnonymous; when AllowAnonymous=true (anonymous auth enabled), requireLogin=false.
findings.effect: Same-instance DoS: goroutine exhaustion and memory pressure from concurrent unique hash requests. LRU cache bounded at 2000 entries mitigates but does not prevent burst DoS. Each cache miss triggers outbound HTTP.
preconditions: [auth.anonymous] enabled=true in grafana.ini
findings.remediation: Change reqSignedIn to reqSignedInNoAnonymous on the /avatar/:hash route at api.go:605 (sets ReqNoAnonymous=true, making requireLogin=true even when anonymous auth is enabled).

findings.title: K8s API Snapshot Delete-by-Key Missing Org Isolation — CVE-2024-1313 Fix Not Ported to K8s Path
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 04 — HIGH confirmed (K8s snapshot API missing org filter)
findings.attacker_control: Authenticated user in any org with ActionSnapshotsDelete permission controls deleteKey URL parameter in DELETE /apis/dashboard.grafana.app/.../snapshots/delete/:deleteKey
findings.runtime: Go server process — K8s API aggregation layer
findings.trust_boundary: TB-9 (K8s API aggregation). Org isolation boundary: CVE-2024-1313 fix applied to legacy REST API (api.go checks snapshot.OrgID != c.OrgID) but NOT ported to K8s API routes.go path which performs RBAC check only.
findings.effect: Cross-tenant: user in Org-B can delete snapshot owned by Org-A if deleteKey is known. deleteKey is 190-bit entropy (high bar) but can leak via API list responses, log files, or shared URLs.
preconditions: Authenticated user with ActionSnapshotsDelete permission
Knowledge of target org's deleteKey (log leak, API response, shared URL)
findings.remediation: After fetching snapshot by deleteKey in routes.go:275, verify snapshot.OrgID == requester's orgID before calling DeleteWithKey. Mirror the org check present in the legacy REST API path.

findings.title: Cloud Migration CancelSnapshot Missing OrgID Parameter Allows Cross-Org Operation (CVE-2024-9476)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 02 — confirmed (Cloud Migration cross-org analysis)
findings.attacker_control: Admin user in any org controls session_uid and snapshot_uid in POST /api/cloudmigration/migration/:uid/snapshot/:snapshotUid/cancel
findings.runtime: Go server process — cloud migration service and database layer
findings.trust_boundary: TB-8 (Cloud Migration). Org isolation: CancelSnapshot(ctx, sessUid, snapshotUid) has no orgID parameter; SQL UPDATE cloud_migration_snapshot WHERE session_uid=? AND uid=? has no org_id constraint.
findings.effect: Cross-tenant: Admin in Org-B can cancel an in-flight migration snapshot owned by Org-A by knowing/obtaining session_uid and snapshot_uid (both UUIDs — globally unique but the WHERE clause has no org-scoping).
preconditions: Admin-level access (ActionMigrationSnapshotCreate) in any org
Knowledge of target org's session_uid and snapshot_uid
findings.remediation: Add orgID to CancelSnapshot service interface signature. Propagate orgID through implementation to UpdateSnapshot. Add AND org_id=? to all UPDATE WHERE clauses in UpdateSnapshot at xorm_store.go:228.

findings.title: Image Renderer ServeFile Without Directory Confinement Check (Chain with JWT Forgery)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 03 — confirmed (ServeFile no confinement, confirmed chain with H2 JWT forgery)
findings.attacker_control: Authenticated user with render access. Full exploit requires forging renderer JWT with default token '-' to control result.FilePath in renderer HTTP response body, then http.ServeFile exposes the attacker-specified path.
findings.runtime: Go server process — render API handler serving files from result.FilePath
findings.trust_boundary: TB-7 (Plugin/Renderer boundary). With default renderer secret '-', the renderer process is effectively untrusted. No filepath.Rel confinement check before http.ServeFile(c.Resp, c.Req, result.FilePath) at render.go:122.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege: chained with H2 (JWT forgery with default token '-'), attacker can read arbitrary server files via http.ServeFile. Standalone risk is low (getNewFilePath generates random path under normal operation) but no defense-in-depth assertion exists.
preconditions: Authenticated user with render access
Default renderer_token '-' configured (enables JWT forgery chain)
findings.phase8_priority: HIGH — exploit chain with JWT forgery (H2)
findings.remediation: Add filepath.Clean confinement check before http.ServeFile: verify result.FilePath is prefixed by expected temp directory. Example: if !strings.HasPrefix(filepath.Clean(result.FilePath), expectedTempDir) { return error }.

findings.title: Cloud Migration SSRF via GMS-Controlled Presigned Upload URL (H4 — PoC Executed)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 02 — HIGH confirmed + EXECUTED (H4: full 4-stage SSRF chain with snapshot_part_*.bin exfiltrated in live Grafana 12.3.2 Docker environment)
findings.attacker_control: Compromised GMS returns presigned URL pointing to internal network endpoint (169.254.169.254/IMDS, VPC CIDR, localhost) in response to Grafana's snapshot upload request
findings.runtime: Go server process — cloud migration GMS client performing HTTP PUT to GMS-provided URL with migration snapshot data
findings.trust_boundary: TB-8 (Cloud Migration external control plane). GMS-provided presigned URL flows into HTTP PUT destination without any URL validation.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege + SSRF: migration snapshot data (datasource credentials, dashboards) exfiltrated to internal network destination. Enables AWS IMDS credential theft, internal service discovery, and full snapshot data exfiltration.
preconditions: Admin initiates cloud migration
Attacker has compromised GMS or can MitM GMS API traffic (requires network position or GMS server compromise)
findings.phase8_priority: HIGH — PoC executed in live environment
findings.remediation: Before HTTP PUT, validate presigned URL host against allowlist of expected storage domains (*.amazonaws.com, *.blob.core.windows.net, *.storage.googleapis.com). Reject RFC1918 addresses, link-local (169.254.0.0/16), and loopback destinations.

findings.title: Plugin Zip Symlink Traversal via Unresolved Intermediate Symlink Chain
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 03 — MEDIUM (plugin zip path confusion; CodeQL go/unsafe-unzip-symlink confirmed)
findings.attacker_control: Malicious plugin author crafts plugin zip with multi-level symlink chain where an intermediate directory component is itself a symlink pointing outside the plugin directory; admin installs the plugin
findings.runtime: Go server process — plugin installer filesystem operations (os.Symlink)
findings.trust_boundary: TB-7 (Plugin system boundary). isSymlinkRelativeTo() uses filepath.Clean (textual path normalization) but does NOT call os.Lstat to resolve actual intermediate symlinks. A directory component in dstPath that is a symlink pointing outside the plugin directory bypasses the containment check.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege (admin-only trigger): arbitrary file write outside plugin directory. Supply chain attack vector: malicious plugin author does not require admin collusion — only needs plugin to pass catalog review.
preconditions: Admin installs malicious plugin from untrusted source or compromised plugin catalog
Grafana process has write permissions at target path
findings.downgrade_note: Downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM: admin must install the plugin. However, supply chain risk is real — plugin catalog compromise is a valid threat actor in this audit's threat model.
findings.remediation: In isSymlinkRelativeTo() (fs.go:165), resolve all intermediate path components via filepath.EvalSymlinks before calling filepath.Rel. Alternatively, after os.Symlink, call filepath.EvalSymlinks on the created symlink to verify the resolved path is still under basePath.

findings.title: Potential SQL Injection in Legacy Dashboard K8s API SQL Access Layer — Requires Call-Site Verification
findings.codeql_slice: SINK-012 (CodeQL taint from provisioning/request.go and web/binding.go)
findings.codeql_reachability: reachable (CodeQL taint path confirmed from HTTP binding to SQL execution)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Not independently confirmed by probe teams — Phase 8 call-site manual verification required
findings.attacker_control: Authenticated user with dashboard read access; CodeQL traces taint from web/binding.go (HTTP request parameter binding) through to sql_dashboards.go:151 executeQuery call
findings.runtime: Go server process — K8s API aggregation dashboard SQL access
findings.trust_boundary: TB-9 (K8s API) to database layer. executeQuery(ctx, query, args...) uses parameterized args for user values in normal cases, but if the query string itself is constructed with user input (not just args), SQL injection is possible.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege: potential SQL injection enabling data extraction beyond the authenticated user's authorized scope across dashboard/dashboard_tag/dashboard_version tables.
preconditions: Authenticated user with dashboard read access
Query string constructed with user-controlled concatenation at specific call site (requires Phase 8 verification)
findings.verdict: KEEP — conditional on Phase 8 call-site verification. CodeQL taint path is confirmed; manual inspection required to determine whether query string contains user-controlled concatenation vs purely parameterized args.
findings.confidence: MEDIUM — CodeQL confirmed taint path; parameterization at specific call site not verified
findings.phase8_priority: MEDIUM — VERIFY FIRST before promoting to final report
findings.remediation: Audit all callers that construct the query string passed to executeQuery(). Ensure all user-controlled values flow through the args variadic parameter, not the query string itself.

findings.title: Plugin Readme Rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML Without DOMPurify Sanitization (Supply Chain XSS)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Not independently confirmed by probe teams
findings.attacker_control: Malicious plugin author controls plugin.details.readme HTML content served from grafana.com plugin catalog; admin views plugin details page triggering rendering
findings.runtime: Browser JavaScript engine — React rendering pipeline (dangerouslySetInnerHTML)
findings.trust_boundary: Browser XSS boundary: grafana.com plugin catalog (external trust boundary) to admin browser session. 13 of 19 dangerouslySetInnerHTML usages in plugin admin UI lack DOMPurify sanitization.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege: XSS in admin browser session enables session token theft, RBAC manipulation, org admin account takeover, and potential persistence via admin API calls.
preconditions: Admin views plugin details page in Grafana UI
Malicious plugin with XSS payload in readme field present in grafana.com plugin catalog (supply chain attack)
findings.phase8_priority: MEDIUM — supply chain XSS; admin-only trigger but cross-privilege effect
findings.remediation: Apply DOMPurify.sanitize() to plugin.details.readme before rendering via dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Audit and remediate remaining 12 unsanitized dangerouslySetInnerHTML usages across the plugin admin UI component tree.

findings.title: Generic OAuth: ID Token Claims Extracted Without Signature Validation by Default (OIDC Core 1.0 Violation)
findings.codeql_slice: DFD-1 (auth pipeline — no dedicated CodeQL slice)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 01 — HIGH confirmed (Generic OAuth no signature verification by default, M4)
findings.attacker_control: Attacker with MITM capability on OAuth exchange (non-HTTPS IdP endpoint, compromised OAuth provider, or rogue OAuth server) controls the ID token payload. When validate_id_token=false (default), the signature field is ignored entirely.
findings.runtime: Go server process — OAuth login flow (generic_oauth connector)
findings.trust_boundary: IdP boundary (TB-2 authentication). The security assumption is that ID tokens are signed by the IdP; when signature validation is skipped, this trust assumption is broken and the token becomes forgeable.
findings.effect: Cross-user: attacker forges ID token claiming any email address, impersonating any Grafana user including GrafanaAdmin. Enables full account takeover without credentials. Affects generic_oauth, gitlab_oauth (line 295), okta_oauth (line 135), google_oauth (line 265).
preconditions: Generic OAuth connector configured with an IdP that returns ID tokens
validate_id_token = false (default) OR jwk_set_url not configured
Attacker can modify the ID token (MITM on non-HTTPS OAuth endpoint, rogue IdP, or compromised OAuth provider)
findings.remediation: Change default of validate_id_token to true and make jwk_set_url required when ID tokens are used for user identity. Emit a deprecation warning when validate_id_token=false is explicitly configured. Apply consistent enforcement across generic_oauth, gitlab_oauth, okta_oauth, google_oauth connectors.

findings.title: OIDC ID Token Validation Omits exp/iss/aud Claims After Signature Verification (OIDC Core 1.0 §3.1.3.7 Violation)
findings.codeql_slice: DFD-1 (auth pipeline — no dedicated CodeQL slice)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 01 — HIGH confirmed (OIDC missing claim validation, M1)
findings.attacker_control: Attacker possesses a previously-valid (now-expired or wrong-audience) ID token from a prior login session or from a different application sharing the same IdP. Token is cryptographically valid (signing key not yet rotated).
findings.runtime: Go server process — OIDC token validation (social_base.go validateIDTokenSignatureWithURLs)
findings.trust_boundary: IdP boundary. validateIDTokenSignatureWithURLs() verifies cryptographic signature (Requirement 6 of OIDC Core 1.0 §3.1.3.7) but omits exp (Requirement 9), iss (Requirement 2), and aud (Requirement 3) checks. Verified claims map is returned without temporal or audience validation.
findings.effect: Cross-user and cross-privilege: expired tokens become permanently valid for re-authentication (defeated token revocation); cross-audience tokens (intended for different relying parties on same IdP) authenticate to Grafana, enabling user impersonation.
preconditions: validate_id_token = true AND jwk_set_url configured (non-default; requires explicit operator configuration)
Attacker possesses a previously-valid ID token whose signing key is still in the IdP's JWKS
findings.remediation: After validateIDTokenSignatureWithURLs(), enforce: (1) exp claim present and current time is before expiry, (2) iss claim exactly matches configured issuer URL, (3) aud claim contains the configured client_id. Use go-jose/v4 built-in claims validation with a properly populated Expected struct.

findings.title: JWT Auth Service: Tokens Without exp Claim Accepted as Permanently Valid
findings.codeql_slice: DFD-1 (JWT auth path — no dedicated CodeQL slice)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 01 — HIGH confirmed (JWT null-exp bypass; variants at M3, M15, M16, M17)
findings.attacker_control: Attacker with the HMAC key (or using a JWT service that issues tokens without exp) crafts or obtains a JWT omitting the exp claim. Token is indefinitely valid until signing key rotation.
findings.runtime: Go server process — JWT authentication (jwt/validation.go validateClaims)
findings.trust_boundary: TB-2 authentication gate. When exp key is absent from JWT payload, registeredClaims.Expiry remains nil; go-jose/v4 Validate() only enforces expiry when Expiry != nil. No default ExpectClaims requires exp presence.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege: a leaked JWT without exp becomes a permanent credential. Defeats time-bounded access control, token revocation, and key rotation procedures (key must be actively rotated to invalidate the token).
preconditions: auth.jwt.enabled = true
JWT is cryptographically valid (correctly signed)
JWT payload does not contain the exp key
No expect_claims configuration forces exp to be present (defaults to empty)
findings.remediation: In validateClaims() (validation.go), explicitly reject tokens where the exp key is absent from the claims map. Alternatively use go-jose/v4 with a require-exp option or validate registeredClaims.Expiry != nil after parsing.

findings.title: Datasource Proxy Forwards Connection Header and Listed Hop-by-Hop Headers to Backend (RFC 7230 §6.1 Violation)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Probe Team 02 — confirmed (proxy path differential / header injection analysis)
findings.attacker_control: Authenticated user with datasources:query RBAC permission controls all request headers including Connection and any headers it lists in GET /api/datasources/proxy/uid/:uid/*
findings.runtime: Go server process — datasource proxy (httputil.ReverseProxy via wrapDirector -> PrepareProxyRequest)
findings.trust_boundary: TB-5 (Datasource Proxy) to TB-6 (External Datasources). PrepareProxyRequest() strips 5 specific headers but does NOT parse the Connection header or remove the hop-by-hop headers it lists. Plugin proxy path correctly implements removeConnectionHeaders() and removeHopByHopHeaders() (client.go:319-372) but datasource proxy path lacks this.
findings.effect: Cross-privilege within backend: authenticated Viewer with datasources:query injects internal authentication headers (via Connection-listed headers) that the backend datasource trusts for privilege elevation. Attacker gains elevated access within the backend system (InfluxDB admin, internal API admin).
preconditions: Authenticated user with datasources:query RBAC permission (default for any role that can query datasources)
Backend datasource trusts specific custom headers for internal authentication or privilege elevation
findings.remediation: In PrepareProxyRequest() (proxyutil.go:26-48), add Connection header parsing and removal of all listed hop-by-hop headers. Mirror the implementation in pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go:319-372 (removeConnectionHeaders + removeHopByHopHeaders).

findings.title: Grafana Live WebSocket Origin Check Unconditionally Allows Empty Origin — Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking Risk
findings.codeql_slice: EP-014 (WebSocket Live endpoint — no dedicated CodeQL slice)
findings.probe_team_confirmation: Not independently confirmed by probe teams
findings.attacker_control: Attacker-controlled page causes victim's authenticated browser to make cross-origin WebSocket connection to /api/live/ws. Origin header is absent due to reverse proxy stripping, non-browser client, or SSE-to-WS bridging.
findings.runtime: Go server process — Grafana Live WebSocket upgrade (Centrifuge library)
findings.trust_boundary: Browser same-origin policy boundary. getCheckOriginFunc() at live.go:538-539 returns true unconditionally when origin == "". This bypasses all configured allowed_origins patterns. pushws/ws.go:54-58 has the same pattern.
findings.effect: Cross-user: attacker's page receives real-time data pushed over victim's Live WebSocket channel (metric streams, alert state updates, dashboard events). Requires victim to be authenticated via session cookie (cookie-based CSWSH).
preconditions: Grafana Live active (default when Live feature is enabled)
Authentication via session cookie
Origin header absent or stripped (reverse proxy stripping, non-browser client, SSE-to-WS bridge)
findings.remediation: Remove the unconditional return true for empty Origin in getCheckOriginFunc() (live.go:538-539). When Origin is empty, either reject the connection or require an explicit configuration allowlist entry for non-browser clients. Apply same fix to pushws/ws.go:54-58.

entry_points_not_in_phase3_dfd_slices.note: SAST-012 path injection finding dropped: static.go:143 uses http.FileSystem.Open() (virtual FS abstraction), not OS filesystem. Path traversal to OS level blocked at FileSystem layer. No DFD slice covers this endpoint; Phase 8 may perform additional verification if needed.
Enterprise-only SCIM endpoint not in Phase 3 DFD slices. CVE-2025-41115 confirmed patched. No OSS-accessible gap remains in this audit.
Server-local provisioning. Attacker requires server filesystem access, equivalent to code execution. Dropped per local tooling drop criterion.
Not in Phase 3 DFD slices but covered by SPEC-GAP-006 (empty Origin bypass). Promoted to enriched findings as EF-014.

sinks_mapping_to_unmodeled_high_risk_flo.note: X-DS-Authorization from client request forwarded as Authorization to backend datasource. This is a separate concern from EF-013 (hop-by-hop headers). Covered by M5 (xDS auth credential injection) from prior audit. Phase 8 should verify whether this flow enables cross-datasource credential injection beyond intended design.

CVE-2026-21722 annotation timerange bypass. SLICE-003 documents this as reachable_from_unauthenticated=true with CRITICAL risk. Covered in prior audit as H1. Not in current SAST candidates but remains a live concern if the prior audit patch was not applied to this commit. Phase 8 should cross-reference patch status.

Dropped as SAST-009 FP. However, the presence of UnsafeClaimsWithoutVerification in any part of the authentication package deserves a Phase 8 note: the Test() function is a router/classifier, and future refactoring that moves authentication logic into or near this path must not rely on the unverified claims output.
